title.en: On the Wire: The bh Elements and IRM Action Frame
title.zh: 线上格式：bh 的信息元素与 IRM 动作帧

blocks.en: Once you accept the core problem the bh amendment solves — an AP must keep recognising a station even as that station rotates its MAC address — a very practical question follows: what bytes actually carry that recognition over the air? An idea like "give me a stable identity that survives a MAC change" is useless until the standard defines concrete containers for it. This lesson opens those containers. The mental model is simple: the new elements are the nouns a station hands over when it joins, and the IRM Action frame is the verb it uses, mid-session, to update its future identity.
blocks.zh: 一旦你接受 bh 修正案要解决的核心问题——即便站点不断轮换自己的 MAC 地址，AP 也必须持续地认出它——紧接着就有一个很现实的问题：在空口上，究竟是哪些字节承载着这种“认出”？像“给我一个能在 MAC 变更后依然有效的稳定身份”这样的想法，在标准为它定义出具体的容器之前都是空谈。本课就来打开这些容器。心智模型很简单：这些新的信息元素是站点入网时交出的“名词”，而 IRM 动作帧则是它在会话过程中用来更新自己未来身份的“动词”。

blocks.en: Three new elements do the noun's job. The Device ID element (9.4.2.316) carries an opaque device ID the AP assigns and the STA presents on its next visit; when the AP sends it, a Device ID Status field reports Recognized / Not Recognized / Not Applicable. The IRM element (9.4.2.317) — IRM stands for Identifiable Random MAC — carries a 6-octet MAC address from the non-AP STA to the AP; sent the other way, the address is absent and only an IRM Status (Recognized / Not Recognized) remains. The Measurement ID element (9.4.2.318) is the slimmest: just a measurement ID the STA echoes back in Probe Requests so a measuring AP can correlate them. All three are Element ID 255 extensions, sharing the common Element ID / Length / Element ID Extension header defined in 9.4.2.1.
blocks.zh: 承担“名词”职责的是三个新元素。Device ID 元素（9.4.2.316）携带一个由 AP 分配、站点在下次到访时出示的不透明设备 ID；当该元素由 AP 发出时，会带一个 Device ID Status 字段，报告“已识别 / 未识别 / 不适用”。IRM 元素（9.4.2.317）——IRM 即 Identifiable Random MAC（可识别随机 MAC）——在非 AP 站点发往 AP 时携带一个 6 字节的 MAC 地址；反方向发送时，地址字段缺省，只保留一个 IRM Status（已识别 / 未识别）。Measurement ID 元素（9.4.2.318）最为精简：仅是一个测量 ID，站点会在 Probe Request 中把它回带，便于做测量的 AP 将这些帧关联起来。这三者都是 Element ID 为 255 的扩展元素，共用 9.4.2.1 中定义的通用 Element ID / Length / Element ID Extension 头部。

caption.en: The three bh elements, each an Element ID 255 extension. Status fields (and the IRM address itself) appear only in the AP-to-STA / STA-to-AP direction the standard specifies.
caption.zh: 三个 bh 元素，均为 Element ID 255 的扩展元素。状态字段（以及 IRM 地址本身）只在标准规定的 AP→STA / STA→AP 方向上出现。

blocks.en: Where do these nouns ride? At join time, mostly. The Device ID and IRM elements are inserted into both the Association Request frame body (9.3.3.5) and the Association Response frame body (9.3.3.6) — optionally present when FILS authentication is in use. The Measurement ID element rides in the Probe Request frame body (9.3.3.9), present when Device ID is activated, so a STA that scans with a random Address 2 can still be tied to an earlier exchange. And the Authentication frame format (9.3.3.11) is touched too, with the bh changes folded into the table that governs which fields and elements appear per authentication step — the same table that already orchestrates PASN. In short, the elements slot into the very frames you met when learning how a station joins.
blocks.zh: 这些“名词”坐在哪些帧里？主要在入网时。Device ID 与 IRM 元素被插入到关联请求帧体（9.3.3.5）和关联响应帧体（9.3.3.6）之中——在使用 FILS 认证时可选出现。Measurement ID 元素则乘坐在探测请求帧体（9.3.3.9）里，当设备 ID 机制激活时出现，这样即便站点用随机的 Address 2 进行扫描，也仍能与此前的交互关联起来。认证帧格式（9.3.3.11）同样被改动，bh 的修改被并入那张决定每个认证步骤出现哪些字段与元素的表格——正是那张本就在编排 PASN 的表。简而言之，这些元素恰好嵌进了你在学习站点如何入网时见过的那些帧。

blocks.en: How does a STA even know the AP supports this? The RSNXE — the Extended RSN Capabilities element (9.4.2.240) — gets two new bits: bit 16 Device ID Support and bit 17 IRM Support. A peer sets the bit to 1 when the matching dot11 capability is activated. That single advertised bit is what lets the two sides agree to play the recognition game before any element is exchanged.
blocks.zh: 站点又是如何知道 AP 支持这一套机制的？RSNXE——即扩展 RSN 能力元素（9.4.2.240）——新增了两个比特位：bit 16 为 Device ID Support，bit 17 为 IRM Support。当对应的 dot11 能力被激活时，一端会把该位置 1。正是这一个被广而告之的比特位，让双方在交换任何元素之前就能就“玩这套识别游戏”达成一致。

blocks.en: Now the verb. The elements settle identity at join time, but a station rotates its MAC mid-session — so it needs a way to tell the AP about a future address without re-associating. That is the IRM Action frame (9.6.36). It lives under a new Action Category, value 39 = IRM, registered in 9.4.1.11; the frame is Robust but not group-addressed-privacy. Right after the Category byte comes a one-octet IRM Action field that picks one of two jobs (9.6.36.1).
blocks.zh: 现在轮到“动词”。元素在入网时确定了身份，但站点会在会话过程中轮换自己的 MAC——因此它需要一种方式，在不重新关联的前提下把未来要用的地址告诉 AP。这就是 IRM 动作帧（9.6.36）。它隶属于一个新的动作类别（Action Category），取值 39 = IRM，在 9.4.1.11 中登记；该帧是 Robust 的，但不属于组寻址隐私类。紧跟在 Category 字节之后，是一个 1 字节的 IRM Action 字段，用来在两项职责中择一（9.6.36.1）。

summary.en: Duplicate vs New IRM — the exact two frame formats
summary.zh: Duplicate 与 New IRM——两种帧的精确格式

blocks.en: Table 9-658a defines the IRM Action field: value 0 = Duplicate IRM, value 1 = New IRM, 2-255 reserved. The Duplicate IRM frame (9.6.36.2, Figure 9-1322a) is just two octets — Category (1) + IRM Action (1) — and carries no address; it re-asserts that the STA intends to keep using the same identifiable random address it already registered. The New IRM frame (9.6.36.3, Figure 9-1322b) appends a 6-octet IRM field — Category (1) + IRM Action (1) + IRM (6) — and that MAC address is the fresh one the STA will roll to. So the difference is literally one field: New IRM names a new address, Duplicate IRM names none because nothing changes. Both reference the Category value registered as code 39 in Table 9-81 (9.4.1.11), and the AP-side recognition is reported back through the IRM Status field defined with the IRM element in 9.4.2.317.
blocks.zh: 表 9-658a 定义了 IRM Action 字段：取值 0 = Duplicate IRM，取值 1 = New IRM，2–255 保留。Duplicate IRM 帧（9.6.36.2，图 9-1322a）仅有两个字节——Category（1）+ IRM Action（1）——不携带任何地址；它用于重新声明：站点打算继续使用它此前已登记的那个可识别随机地址。New IRM 帧（9.6.36.3，图 9-1322b）则追加了一个 6 字节的 IRM 字段——Category（1）+ IRM Action（1）+ IRM（6）——其中的这个 MAC 地址，就是站点即将切换过去的全新地址。所以两者的差别实实在在只有一个字段：New IRM 给出一个新地址，而 Duplicate IRM 不给地址，因为什么都没变。两者都引用了表 9-81（9.4.1.11）中登记为代码 39 的 Category 值；AP 侧的识别结果则通过与 IRM 元素一同在 9.4.2.317 中定义的 IRM Status 字段回报。

9.4.2.240 RSNXE bits 16/17 (Device ID Support / IRM Support); 9.4.2.316 Device ID element; 9.4.2.317 IRM element; 9.4.2.318 Measurement ID element
9.6.36.1 IRM Action field table; 9.6.36.2 Duplicate IRM frame; 9.6.36.3 New IRM frame (adds 6-octet IRM)
